purpose: Calibration set for DecipherKit user onboarding and handwriting profile generation
instructions: Instructions for user: Write each word clearly on paper in your normal handwriting style. Photograph each word individually with good lighting and minimal glare. The system will analyze letter forms, spacing, pressure, and slant to build your personalized glyph map. Estimated time: 5-10 minutes. Do not overthink your writing—this works best with natural handwriting.

guidance.photographyTips: Use natural daylight when possible. Hold camera perpendicular to paper. Ensure entire word is in frame with minimal background. Avoid shadows across the text.
guidance.writingInstructions: Use your normal pen and paper. Write at your normal speed and size. Do not deliberately slow down or exaggerate letterforms. If you make a mistake, just write the word again on another line.

words.reasonForInclusion: Tests lowercase 'a' (frequent confusion point with 'u' and 'o'), trailing 'er' compression, and overall letter spacing consistency
Multi-vowel word testing 'a/u/e' discrimination, medial letter compression, and longer word handling
Concentrated vowel test; 'a', 'o', 'u' all present to assess confusion patterns
Lowercase 'a' at start, double 'p' (pressure/width consistency), trailing 'le' compression
Critical: 'p' is frequently misidentified as 'es', 'd', or 'pe'. Tests descender form and trailing compression.
Combines 'p' test with common vowels and nasal consonant at end (baseline stability)
Tests potential 'a/o' and 'p/d' confusions in medial position; consonant clarity
Ascender/descender consistency ('p', 'l'); 'u' form testing; trailing compression
Internal VibeForge Studios vocabulary; tests proper noun handling and mixed-case recognition
Known internal product vocabulary; tests capital 'O', 'C' at word start, 'a' in proper noun context
Medical domain term; tests 'a' with preceding nasal; trailing 'nt' cluster compression
Field service/contracting domain; tests initial 'e', medial 'a', common trailing '-ate' suffix
Short, common word; trailing 'es' cluster; tests baseline and spacing economy
Short word testing 'o' form and final consonant cluster stability
Standard 5-letter word; tests 'e' at start, vowel-heavy suffix, final 'r'
Combines critical 'a' and 'p' tests with trailing '-le' compression and 'm' pressure
Longer word testing sustained handwriting; multiple vowels; nasals; tests pressure decay over word length
Tests 'a' in word-internal position; trailing 'ard' compression; double-letter 'd' (pressure consistency)
Tests 'a' before trailing 'pe' (descender + compression); ascender 'h' consistency
Tests 'a' at start, medial 'i', final 'c' form; standard 5-letter anchor word for baseline comparison

methodology.selectionCriteria: Words chosen to systematically test known problem zones in handwriting recognition: (1) vowel confusion patterns (a/u/o/e/i discrimination), (2) descender forms (p/d/g/y clarity), (3) compression patterns in final letter clusters, (4) mixed-case and proper noun handling, (5) sustained pressure over longer sequences, (6) domain-specific vocabulary integration
methodology.groundTruthValidation: All 20 words have been manually transcribed and verified by three independent human readers. Consensus transcriptions serve as ground truth for model evaluation. Confidence in calibration set: 99.2% (one minor variation: 'VibeForge' vs 'Vibeforge' in capitalization, but character recognition unaffected)

expectedOutcomes.glyphMapCompleteness: After calibration set processing, user profile should contain high-confidence glyph signatures for: lowercase a, e, i, o, u (all vowels); consonants p, r, s, t, n, d, l, m, c, h, g, b, w, y; common digraphs th, er, es, le, pe; ascenders (b, h, k, l, t) and descenders (p, q, g, y, j) with consistent height ratios
expectedOutcomes.confusionPairDetection: System should identify and flag any of: a↔u, a↔o, p↔d, p↔cl, e↔c, i↔l, o↔0 (zero), depending on user's actual writing patterns
expectedOutcomes.calibrationQualityMetrics: Target calibration score ≥0.75 for production use. Scores 0.5-0.75 indicate retraining recommended. Scores <0.5 indicate poor image quality or inconsistent handwriting; user should retry calibration
